99.900 blocks until the next Bitcoin halving.

Sounds far away.

Then you remember how fast crypto cycles start feeling compressed once momentum comes back.

The default assumption right now is that AI will make crypto way less secure.

More bugs found.
More exploits automated.
More attack surfaces.

Vitalik thinks the long term outcome could actually be the opposite.

Many people have claimed that with AI-assisted bug finding, secure code (and hence trustless anything) will be impossible.

I have a much more optimistic take, and AI-assisted formal verification is a major part of the reason why:

BTC looked strong above $80K .

But underneath, the structure had been getting shaky for days.

ETF outflows picked up.
Leverage got crowded near resistance.
Yields kept climbing.

Then one fast move below $77K wiped out overleveraged longs all at once.
